Package.Name: Conversion
Package.Summary: Test conversion policies, collection handling, enums, temporal values, and structured failure paths.

Fields.ValueHelp: Enter arrays as JSON. The expression view renders them as supported C#-style literals.
Fields.MaximumItemsHelp: This example accepts 1–500 items. ConversionContext.Strict retains the package default of 10,000.

Result.Title: Result
Result.Empty: Run the current input to inspect the conversion result.

Tips.Title: What to notice
Tips.Parser: The expression editor accepts a small grammar and never compiles or evaluates arbitrary C#.
Tips.Allowlist: Runtime target names resolve through a fixed allowlist, not arbitrary reflection.
Tips.Bounds: Input, output, depth, and collection limits are enforced by the example.
Tips.Source: Source literals are parsed into a small set of supported scalar and array values before conversion.
Tips.Failures: Expected conversion failures preserve stable package codes and value paths.
Tips.Policies: Set culture and loss policies explicitly at application boundaries.
Tips.Strict: Strict defaults are invariant and reject data loss.
Tips.Saturation: Saturation is explicit; ordinary overflow fails.
Tips.Culture: Culture changes textual parsing, not deterministic result formatting.
Tips.Collection: Collection conversion stops at the first failed element.
Tips.FailurePath: Failure paths identify the exact item or member that failed.
Tips.NoSerializer: The converter has no object or JSON serializer fallback.

Purpose.Title: Purpose
Purpose.Body: Pocok.Conversion replaces scattered Convert.ChangeType calls and serializer-based coercion with immutable policies and explicit results.

QuickStart.Title: Quick start
QuickStart.Body: Choose a sample, edit the input or policies, then run it. The example invokes ValueConverter.Default directly.

Targets.Title: Supported targets
Targets.Body: The example allowlist includes scalar values, signed and unsigned numbers, Guid, temporal types, local enums and flags, and selected arrays.

Context.Title: ConversionContext
Context.Body: ConversionContext captures culture, loss, overflow, null, enum, boolean, temporal, depth, and collection policies in one immutable value.

Policies.Title: Policies
Policies.Body: Strict rejects overflow, fractions, numeric booleans, undefined enums, and non-round-trip temporal text unless a policy explicitly allows them.

Failures.Title: Structured failures
Failures.Body: ConversionFailure exposes a stable code, safe message, and value path. Expected failures remain package results rather than host errors.

Collections.Title: Collections
Collections.Body: Selected enumerable sources convert item by item, stop at the first failure, and report an indexed path. Strings are treated as scalar text.

Enums.Title: Enums and flags
Enums.Body: Named values are validated. Flags combinations are accepted only when every bit is declared and the selected enum policy permits flags.

Temporal.Title: Temporal values
Temporal.Body: RoundTrip accepts invariant round-trip text. CultureAware delegates textual parsing to the selected culture.

Concurrency.Title: Concurrency
Concurrency.Body: ValueConverter.Default and immutable contexts can be shared safely. Showcase input, result, and cancellation state remain scoped to the current user circuit.

Boundaries.Title: Boundaries
Boundaries.Body: The general conversion API does not claim trimming or NativeAOT support, does not resolve arbitrary types, and has no serializer fallback.

Production.Title: Production notes
Production.Body: Choose policies at trusted boundaries, preserve failure codes, cap collections, and map untrusted target names only through an allowlist.

Assist.Convert: Insert an approved generic Convert call.
Assist.Typeof: Select a runtime target from the fixed type allowlist.
Assist.NamedArgument: Insert an approved named context argument.
Assist.Target: Insert a supported target type alias.
Assist.Policy: Insert a supported policy enum value.
Assist.Runtime: Runtime target selection remains fixed and allowlisted.

Boundaries.Title: Boundaries
Boundaries.NoCompilation: No C# compilation or expression evaluation.
Boundaries.FixedTargets: Only fixed target types and policy enums.
Boundaries.BoundedInput: Bounded code, input, output, depth, and collection size.
Boundaries.NoSerializer: No object or JSON serializer fallback.

Source.Title: Source documentation
Source.Body: Package documentation for the public contract, policies, and boundaries.

strict-integer.Name: Strict integer
strict-integer.Description: Convert text to an integer with strict invariant policies.

saturating-byte.Name: Saturating byte
saturating-byte.Description: Clamp 300 to the byte maximum with an explicit overflow policy.

german-decimal.Name: German decimal
german-decimal.Description: Parse 1.234,5 with the German culture and display the result invariantly.

flags.Name: Flags enum
flags.Description: Convert comma-separated names into a validated flags combination.

collection-success.Name: Collection success
collection-success.Description: Convert a string array into an integer array.

collection-failure.Name: Collection failure path
collection-failure.Description: Stop at the first invalid item and preserve its indexed failure path.

fraction-rejected.Name: Fraction rejected
fraction-rejected.Description: Reject fractional loss when converting 12.7 to an integer.

fraction-rounded.Name: Fraction rounded
fraction-rounded.Description: Round 12.7 with the explicit nearest policy.

numeric-boolean.Name: Numeric boolean
numeric-boolean.Description: Opt into zero-or-one conversion from numbers to booleans.

null-default.Name: Null policy
null-default.Description: Use the target default for null, then compare Preserve and Reject in the builder.

temporal-roundtrip.Name: Temporal round trip
temporal-roundtrip.Description: Parse a DateTimeOffset with the invariant round-trip policy and preserve its offset.

undefined-enum.Name: Undefined enum
undefined-enum.Description: Reject a numeric value that is not declared by the target enum.
